# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
  - name: compute-instance
    type: google::compute/v1beta1/instance
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
      networkInterface0:
        name: global-network-interface
        accessCustomized: true
        network: global/networks/default

  - name: cloud-sql-instance
    type: google::sqladmin/v1beta4/instance
    properties:
      databaseVersion: POSTGRES_12
      region: us-central1
      instanceId: my-cloud-sql-instance
      tier: db-n1-standard-2

  - name: cloud-storage-bucket
    type: google::storage/v1/buckets
    properties:
      name: my-bucket
      storageClass: STANDARD

  - name: load-balancer
    type: google::compute/v1beta1/targetHttpsProxy
    properties:
      target: httpsLoadBalancer
      zone: us-central1-a
      urlMap: $(ref.url-map)
      serviceAccountEmail: projects/my-project-id/serviceAccounts/loadbalancer@my-project-id.iam.gserviceaccount.com

  - name: url-map
    type: google::compute/v1beta1/urlMap
    properties:
      hostRules:
        - hosts:
          - my-domain.com
          pathRules:
            - paths:
              - /*
                service: $(ref.load-balancer)
